summary: ANDROID 🟥 Correct Firmware version for Charge Case but Update available on Software tab after DFU
feature: Android App - Device Settings / Software Update
scenario: After DFU on Charge Case, correct firmware version shown but Update button remains active with same build number, causing immediate DFU failure until app restart
description_clean: After performing a DFU (Device Firmware Update) on the Charge Case, the correct firmware version is displayed but the "Update" button remains available with the same build number. Tapping the update button causes DFU to fail immediately. The proper firmware version only appears after restarting the app.
steps: Perform a DFU (Device Firmware Update) on the Charge Case.
Observe that the correct firmware version is displayed.
Check if the 'Update' button remains available with the same build number.
Observe that the DFU fails immediately.
Observe that the proper firmware version now appears.
expected: After the DFU, the correct firmware version should be displayed, the "Update" button should no longer be available (or show the new build number), and tapping update should not cause a failure. The proper firmware version should appear without needing to restart the app.
actual: After the DFU, the correct firmware version is displayed but the "Update" button remains available with the same build number. Tapping the update button causes DFU to fail immediately. The proper firmware version only appears after restarting the app.
